channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UN announces new plan to counter the surge in antisemitism
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations announced new actions Friday to counter the surge in antisemitism, including encouraging governments to enforce laws against hate crimes and discrimination. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Impeached South Korean president to appear in court hearing to argue against his arrest
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached president will appear at a hearing in a Seoul court on Saturday to oppose a formal arrest over last month’s imposition of martial law, his lawyers said.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel's full Cabinet approves the Gaza ceasefire and hostage release deal
item.description: Israel's Cabinet has approved the Gaza ceasefire deal that would pause the fighting and release dozens of hostages held by militants, along with Palestinians imprisoned by Israel. The approval came early on Saturday after a marathon session by the full Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blessings for dogs? Bring them to Mexico City's cathedral and St. Anthony will do the rest
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Behaving at their best, a dozen dogs attended Mass at Mexico City’s cathedral Friday, waiting for their turn to be doused with holy water. The blessing of the animals is a long-time Catholic tradition celebrated on January 17.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Attorneys for fire victim say utility may have destroyed evidence of what caused deadly LA-area fire
item.description: Attorneys for a woman who is among thousands who lost their homes in the Eaton Fire outside Los Angeles say Southern California Edison crews working to repair and restore power in the area may have destroyed evidence that could help determine what sparked the wildfire.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dearen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the hostages and cease-fire deal between Israel and Hamas set to begin Sunday
item.description: Israel and Hamas finalize a historic cease-fire and hostage deal set to begin Sunday. The agreement includes phased releases, intense negotiations and ongoing security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daughter of one of the oldest Israeli hostages hopes for answers in ceasefire deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sharone Lifschitz is well aware that the odds are against her 84-year-old father. As one of the oldest hostages taken by Hamas, Oded Lifshitz would be among the first to be released under a ceasefire deal expected to begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British PM Starmer visits Auschwitz, vows to fight antisemitism ahead of security talks in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday visited the Nazi German extermination camp at Auschwitz, voicing his “sheer horror” at what he saw there, before holding talks with Poland's leaders on stepping up European defense and tightening Britain's ties with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 lawyers for the late Kremlin foe Alexei Navalny are convicted and sentenced to prison
item.description: PETUSHKI, Russia (AP) — Three lawyers who once represented the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny were convicted by a court Friday as part of the Kremlin’s crackdown on dissent that has reached levels unseen since Soviet times.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Anti-AIDS program in GOP crosshairs over abortion funding
item.description: Republicans are fuming at the State Department after learning that recipients of funding from the legacy program to curb HIV/AIDS across Africa, PEPFAR, performed abortions in violation of U.S. law. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump and Chinese leader Xi talk about trade, fentanyl and TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping discussed trade, fentanyl and TikTok in a phone call Friday, just days before Trump heads back to the White House with vows to impose tariffs and other measures on America's biggest rival.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: South African miner describes horrors for those who spent months underground
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa. (AP) — Dozens of men sat and lay together in small caves, some so shallow they couldn’t stand up. The air was wet and stuffy, and wracking coughs echoed in the confined space. Bodies wrapped in fabric and twine were set aside in rows nearby.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French president in Lebanon to meet the country's new leaders and discuss ceasefire with Israel
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — France’s president began a visit to Lebanon Friday, where he will meet the crisis-hit country’s newly elected leaders, as the nation attempts to recover from the 14-month Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, Hamas ceasefire poised to begin Sunday
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is poised to begin Sunday, after the Israeli security Cabinet on Friday endorsed the deal covering it and the release of hostages and called for the full government to agree to the terms.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia and Iran have a troubled history despite their current alliance
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin hosted his Iranian counterpart Friday for the signing of a broad pact between Moscow and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaza is in ruins, and it's unclear how it will be rebuilt
item.description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are eager to leave miserable tent camps and return to their homes if a long-awaited ceasefire agreement halts the Israel-Hamas war, but many will find there is nothing left and no way to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: Officials say more than 40 Pakistanis are feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off West Africa’s Atlantic coastline.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hungarian minister sanctioned by US 'strengthened' by the penalty, Prime Minister Orbán says
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Sanctions issued by the United States last week against a powerful Hungarian official with close ties to Prime Minister Viktor Orbán have “strengthened” that official's standing within the populist government, Orbán said Friday. Speaking on state radio, Orbán said the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: Israel’s Cabinet has approved a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages that will pause the 15-month war with Hamas for six weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says deal to release hostages held in Gaza has been reached after last minute snags
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Friday a deal to return hostages held in the Gaza Strip has been reached, after his office said earlier there were last minute snags in finalizing a ceasefire that would pause 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Xi's special representative Han Zheng to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: China’s vice president will attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration, according to a Thursday statement from the Foreign Ministry Spokesperson. “At U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Former Bank of England governor Mark Carney enters race to be Canada’s next prime minister
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Mark Carney, the first non-Brit to run the Bank of England since it was founded in 1694 and the former head of Canada’s central bank, said Thursday he is entering the race to be Canada’s next prime minister following the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada sends Trump 'message' with retaliatory tariff talk
item.description: Canada must send a “message” to President-elect Trump on tariffs, one of the country's provincial leaders said Wednesday alongside Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, who described the threatened levies as unnecessary.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Danish prime minister tells Trump Greenland will decide its future
item.description: The Danish prime minister told President-elect Trump that Greenland will decide its future in a call Wednesday, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Pence lands in Taiwan for meetings with president, tech leaders
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence arrived in Taiwan on Thursday, two sources familiar with the trip told The Hill. Pence plans to meet with Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te and Vice President Hsiao Bi-khim, who Pence has known from her time as Taiwan’s representative to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel, Hamas cease-fire held up over renewed debate over Philadelphi security corridor, terrorist exchange
item.description: Old disputes over security concerns involving the Philadelphi corridor have apparently stalled a deal that Israel was set to vote on after the U.S. and Qatar said hostage exchanges would begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu throws curveball into Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is tapping the brakes on claims of success by President Biden, President-elect Trump and partners in the Middle East that a ceasefire and hostage release deal has been achieved to pause 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kirby 'confident' Americans in Gaza will be freed Sunday amid reports of Israel, Hamas deal holdup
item.description: The White House national security communications adviser John Kirby said he is "confident" a deal will be implemented on Sunday despite Israeli claims that Hamas has backtracked on deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu delays Gaza cease-fire vote, accusing Hamas of trying to back out of deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday accused Hamas of backing out of a cease-fire deal to release hostages and bring a pause to more than a year of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mozambique's Chapo sworn in as president after disputed election
item.description: Following months of protests against his disputed election victory, Daniel Chapo of Mozambique's Frelimo party has been sworn in as president at a sparsely attended ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, told the pope Saturday he was a recipient of the medal.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation
